caption: Figure 1: Accuracy–P95 latency scatterplot for all evaluated models. Both axes now use the pre-registered gating metrics: exact-match accuracy (y) and P95 latency (x). Filled markers: Study 1 harmonized benchmark (identical hardware). Open markers (E): Study 2 randomized experiment (neff =60). The shaded green region marks the viable operating zone (accuracy ≥ 0.85, P95 ≤ 2,000 ms). No model enters the viable region. DeepSeek-V3 approaches the accuracy threshold but exceeds the latency gate (P95 = 2,295 ms). Qwen-2.5-3B is Pareto-dominant among self-hosted models.
description2: Accuracy–P95 latency scatterplot plots exact-match accuracy against P95 latency for all evaluated models using pre-registered gating metrics.
description3: Two-panel scatterplot displays accuracy (y-axis) versus P95 latency (x-axis) for various models, with filled and open markers distinguishing study conditions.
questions: What general trends are observed in model accuracy versus latency?
How are the models differentiated based on study and performance markers?
